rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical alkene sulfonation with inorganic sulfites and alcohols to form alkoxysulfonyl-functionalized products (sulfonate esters) via anodic generation of alkoxysulfonyl radicals
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of inorganic sulfite (SO3^2- / HSO3-) to sulfite radical anion (SO3•-), which is trapped by alcohol to give alkoxysulfonyl radical ROSO2•; subsequent anodic oxidation of the C-radical adduct to a carbocation enables polar trapping by alcohol

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical B from addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation C (after anodic SET of B)
β-hydroxy carbon radical D (from addition to homoallyl/allyl alcohol 4)
migrated radical E (after radical functional group migration)
cation F (after anodic oxidation of E)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes inorganic sulfite to SO3•-, which is captured by ROH to give an alkoxysulfonyl radical (ROSO2•). This radical adds to the alkene to give a C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; alcohol (or deprotonation) then delivers the sulfonate ester product, while the cathode reduces protons/ROH to H2.

probe_reagents.name: methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide (6) radical trap/cyclization probe
probe_reagents.observation: cyclized sulfonate ester 7 obtained in 18% yield, indicating an alkoxysulfonyl radical adds to the alkene and the resulting C-radical cyclizes

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted styrene (1bi) radical clock
probe_reagents.observation: ring-opened product 8 (15%) formed alongside difunctionalized 3bi (24%), supporting a radical addition intermediate

probe_reagents.name: trifluoromethyl-substituted styrene (1bj)
probe_reagents.observation: gave difunctionalized product 3bj (37%) and elimination alkene 9 (14%), consistent with cationic intermediate undergoing proton elimination

probe_reagents.name: enamide 1bk
probe_reagents.observation: afforded coupling sulfonate 10 in 84% yield, supporting cationic intermediate formed by anodic oxidation of radical adduct

probe_reagents.name: SO2 gas (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: no oxidation peak in MeCN/MeOH, excluding oxidation of SO2 / alkyl sulfite as radical source

probe_reagents.name: NaHSO3 (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.23 V vs SCE, lower than styrene 1a (1.80 V), indicating preferential anodic oxidation of inorganic sulfite

probe_reagents.name: Na2S2O5 (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: oxidation peak at 1.15 V vs SCE; alkene 4a showed no oxidation peak, supporting sulfite oxidation as initiation

notes: For homoallyl/allyl alcohol substrates 4, a radical functional group migration pathway (D → E → F → 5) is proposed in parallel to the direct radical–polar crossover route used for styrenes. CV experiments rule out direct SO2 or alkyl sulfite oxidation as the radical source.
